subject: Questions about international admission requirements
body: Hello Admissions Team, I am applying as an international student for Fall 2027 and wanted to confirm if IELTS scores are mandatory when I already have SAT verbal scores. Could you also share the deadline for scholarship consideration?
aiDraft:
Hello Maya,

Thank you for your interest in EduMail University. For international applicants, IELTS or TOEFL is generally required unless your prior education was completed in English. SAT verbal scores are helpful but do not replace the English proficiency requirement in most cases.

For scholarship consideration, please submit your full application by December 15.

Best regards,
Admissions Office
summary: International applicant asking about English-language requirements and scholarship timing for Fall 2027.

threadHistory.label: Student inquiry
threadHistory.author: Maya Thompson
threadHistory.body: Hello Admissions Team, I am applying as an international student for Fall 2027 and wanted to confirm if IELTS scores are mandatory when I already have SAT verbal scores. Could you also share the deadline for scholarship consideration?

threadHistory.label: Routing note
threadHistory.author: Admissions triage
threadHistory.body: Assigned to international admissions review because scholarship timing is bundled into the same response.

sourceCitations.excerpt: International applicants must provide IELTS or TOEFL scores unless prior study was completed in English.
Priority scholarship review requires a complete application submitted by December 15.
sourceCitations.reason: Supports the scholarship deadline in the draft.

subject: Installment plan for spring tuition payment
body: Hi Finance Office, I cannot pay the full spring tuition this month. Is there a monthly installment option and what are the late fee rules if one payment is delayed?
aiDraft:
Hello Liam,

Yes, we offer a monthly installment plan for spring tuition. You can enroll through the student billing portal under Payment Plans. Please note that each missed installment may incur a late fee based on our tuition policy.

If needed, we can also connect you with Financial Aid to discuss additional support options.

Regards,
Student Finance Team
summary: Finance inquiry about tuition installments and late-fee exposure for spring billing.

threadHistory.label: Student inquiry
threadHistory.author: Liam Patel
threadHistory.body: Hi Finance Office, I cannot pay the full spring tuition this month. Is there a monthly installment option and what are the late fee rules if one payment is delayed?

threadHistory.label: Approved reply
threadHistory.author: Noah Kim
threadHistory.body: Confirmed installment-plan availability, pointed the student to the billing portal, and called out late-fee policy plus financial-aid escalation.

sourceCitations.excerpt: Students may enroll in a monthly payment plan through the billing portal before the published spring tuition deadline.
sourceCitations.reason: Supports the installment-plan availability in the reply.

subject: Name mismatch on transcript request
body: A transcript request from student ID 224198 was flagged due to a legal name mismatch between SIS and the transcript form. Please review and advise next steps.
staffNote: Potential legal-name exception. Confirm the registrar identity-match policy before replying.

subject: Grade change approval process for capstone course
body: Good afternoon, I need to submit a grade correction for two capstone students due to an LMS export issue. Can you confirm the approval chain and expected processing timeline?
aiDraft:
Dear Professor Cruz,

Thank you for reaching out. Grade correction requests for capstone courses should be submitted via the Academic Records portal. After submission, requests route to your department chair for approval and then to the Registrar for final posting.

Typical processing time is 3-5 business days.

Best,
Academic Operations
summary: Faculty request about grade-correction approvals and turnaround for capstone records.

threadHistory.label: Faculty inquiry
threadHistory.author: Prof. Elena Cruz
threadHistory.body: Good afternoon, I need to submit a grade correction for two capstone students due to an LMS export issue. Can you confirm the approval chain and expected processing timeline?

threadHistory.label: Draft queued
threadHistory.author: Academic Operations
threadHistory.body: Draft created from academic-records SOP. Waiting for assignment before final review.

sourceCitations.excerpt: Grade corrections route to the department chair first and then to the Registrar for posting after approval.

subject: Deadline to drop elective without W grade
body: Hi, could you tell me the last date to drop an elective this semester without receiving a W on my transcript? I am considering a schedule adjustment.
aiDraft:
Hello Noah,

The last date to drop an elective course without receiving a W grade this semester is April 4. You can process the change through the registration portal before 11:59 PM local time.

If you want help evaluating schedule impacts, we recommend speaking with your academic advisor.

Regards,
Registrar Office
summary: Registrar deadline question about course-drop timing and transcript impact.

threadHistory.label: Student inquiry
threadHistory.author: Noah Kim
threadHistory.body: Hi, could you tell me the last date to drop an elective this semester without receiving a W on my transcript? I am considering a schedule adjustment.

threadHistory.label: Approved reply
threadHistory.author: Jordan Lee
threadHistory.body: Confirmed the no-W drop deadline and directed the student to academic advising for schedule planning.

sourceCitations.excerpt: The last day to drop an elective without a W grade is April 4 at 11:59 PM local time.
sourceCitations.reason: Supports the deadline quoted in the approved reply.
